Description: Litoditis marina genome assembly, chromosome: VII
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Nematoda; Chromadorea; Rhabditida; Rhabditina; Rhabditomorpha; Rhabditoidea; Rhabditidae; Litoditis
Assembly: The assembly nrLitMari1.1 is based on PacBio HiFi and Hi-C data derived from pools of individuals generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute's 959 Nematode Genomes project. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, contamination removal via blobtoolkit, and scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Mollusca; Cephalopoda; Coleoidea; Decapodiformes; Sepiida; Sepiolidae; Sepiolinae; Sepiola
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Mollusca; Cephalopoda; Coleoidea; Decapodiformes; Sepiida; Sepiolidae; Sepiolinae; Sepiola
Assembly: The assembly xcSepAtla11.1 is based on 33x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Trichoptera; Integripalpia; Plenitentoria; Limnephiloidea; Limnephilidae; Limnephilinae; Limnephilini; Limnephilus
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Trichoptera; Integripalpia; Plenitentoria; Limnephiloidea; Limnephilidae; Limnephilinae; Limnephilini; Limnephilus
Assembly: The assembly iiLimAuri1.1 is based on 26x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z was assigned based on read coverage statistics. The sample appears to be ZO as no W could be identified.
